news.title: Hegseth says potential $200 billion Iran war spending request could shift: 'Takes money to kill bad guys'
news.summary: President Donald Trump's National Economic Council director Kevin Hassett said Sunday that the war has already cost $12 billion. Hassett: The war has cost the U.S. more than $10 billion so far.

news.title: Micron falls 5% after blowout earnings. CEO says it can supply only a fraction of key customer needs
news.summary: Micron stock is up more than 350% in the past year, however, thanks to a memory supply shortage driven by surging demand for Nvidia's AI chips.

news.title: Traders now see little chance of an interest rate cut this year following Fed decision
news.summary: All of the positive economic talk out of this week's Federal Reserve meeting had a negative impact on investors. The U.S. economy is expected to grow by 0.3 percent this year.

news.title: Uber to invest up to $1.25 billion in EV maker Rivian in deal to launch 50,000 robotaxis
news.summary: Uber plans to invest up to $1.25 billion in Rivian as part of a deal to deploy up to 50,000 robotaxis in several countries.

news.title: Trump's DHS pick Markwayne Mullin advances out of Senate committee
news.summary: President Donald Trump tapped Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., earlier this month to replace Kristi Noem. Mullin is a former governor of Oklahoma.

news.title: How America’s war is turning into a European market nightmare
news.summary: Interest-rate expectations surge for both the Bank of England and European Central Bank. U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran are hurting European markets most of all.

news.title: Alibaba’s stock slips as a big drop in net income overshadows AI progress
news.summary: Alibaba's cloud and AI units delivered another quarter of triple-digit growth. But the company’s costly pivot to rapid e-commerce delivery led to an earnings miss.

news.title: ‘Epic Fury’ has already canceled out Big Beautiful Bill’s tax refunds — even if the Iran war ended today
news.summary: A group of Stanford economists pit tax refunds versus rising gasoline prices. The results aren't good for American households, the economists say. The study was published in the journal American Economic Journal.

news.title: Is the economy really losing jobs? The low number of unemployment filings says no.
news.summary: The U.S. economy is not losing jobs, as the February employment report found. Not according to the low number of people applying for jobless benefits.

news.title: Global oil prices trade at $112 a barrel after Trump threatens to ‘blow up’ the world’s largest gas field
news.summary: Global oil prices are back near their highest levels in nearly four years Thursday. Iran targeted energy production in the Gulf region. Iran has been accused of being a major oil producer in the region.
